title: Tools, Device Commands, PBQs, and Certification Capstone · Guided Evidence Lab
summary: Use software, hardware, discovery, capture, and device evidence to design, implement, secure, operate, and repair a branch network. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of a performance-based branch network capstone while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use ping, traceroute or tracert, nslookup, dig, netstat, ip, ifconfig, ipconfig, arp, and tcpdump with platform-aware interpretation.
Use protocol analyzers and authorized Nmap discovery or service probes with scope, timing, vantage, and false-result awareness.
Use LLDP or CDP and speed tests while accounting for device support, advertisement trust, test path, server, and competing load.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# comptia-network-plus safe operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic browser simulator.
# Add one observation, command, expected signal, and conclusion per activity.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model
steps.instruction:
workshop case — Use a changed workshop case for network tools capstone that tests this competency: defend the module contract. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

What must be established before implementation?
